title: Cot loading and unloading systems having a fixed rail
abstract: In one embodiment, a cot loading and unloading system includes a fixed rail having a track, wherein the fixed rail is operable to be fixed to a floor, and a carriage directly coupled to the track and operable to travel along the fixed rail on the track, wherein the carriage is further operable to travel to an extended position such that at least a portion of the carriage extends beyond a length of the fixed rail.
claims:
Claims 1. A support beam for supporting a carriage of a cot loading and unloading system, the support beam comprising a support beam member operable to translate within a fixed rail that is fixed to a vehicle, and operable to support the carriage beyond a length of the fixed rail when the support beam is in an extended position. 2. The support beam of claim 1, wherein the support beam member is U-shaped. 3. The support beam of claim 1, wherein the support beam member comprises a first support beam member side, a second support beam member side, and a top plate. 4. The support beam of claim 1, further comprising an end block positioned at an end of the support beam, wherein a width of the end block is greater than a width of the support beam member. 5. The support beam of claim 4, further comprising a first locking assembly and a second locking assembly at opposite ends of the end block, wherein the first locking assembly and the second locking assembly are operable to both lock the support beam to the fixed rail and lock the support beam to the carriage. 6. The support beam of claim 5, wherein each of the first locking assembly and the second locking assembly comprises: a channel for receiving a block of the carriage; an actuation button slidably positioned within the channel and operable to receive the block of the carriage; a lock arm coupled to the actuation button; a carriage locking pin partially disposed within the channel; and a pin actuation button extending from a surface of the end block, wherein: the carriage locking pin is biased in an extended position toward the channel; when the pin actuation button contacts a face of the fixed rail: 

 the pin actuation button moves in a direction away from the face of the fixed rail and lifts the carriage locking pin into a retracted position; and an end of the lock arm is disposed within a lock groove of the fixed rail to lock the support beam to the fixed rail; when the block of the carriage enters the channel: the block translates the actuation button in a direction away from the face of the fixed rail to lift the lock arm out of the lock groove of the fixed rail to unlock the support beam from the fixed rail; the carriage pushes the support beam out of the fixed rail such that the pin actuation button is not in contact with the face of the fixed rail; and movement of the pin actuation button in a direction toward the face of the fixed rail causes the carriage locking pin to be lowered into an extended position within the channel such that the carriage locking pin is disposed within a pin receiving groove of the block of the carriage. 7. The support beam of claim 6, wherein: the carriage locking pin has an L-shape and comprises an activation portion and a locking portion; the locking portion extends into the channel; the pin actuation button comprises a ramp surface; when the pin actuation button contacts the face of the fixed rail the ramp surface engages the activation portion to cause the carriage locking pin to be in a retracted state; and when the pin actuation button does not contact the face of the fixed rail, the ramp surface lowers the activation portion to cause the carriage locking pin to be in an extended state. 8. The support beam of claim 1, further comprising an end block comprising an unlock button operable to: unlock a cot from the cot loading and unloading system and unlock the carriage from the fixed rail in a first mode; and unlock the cot from the cot loading and unloading system and unlock the cot from the carriage in a second mode. 9. The support beam of claim 8, further comprising a release rod coupled to the unlock button at a first end and a switch plate at a second end, wherein the switch plate is operable to change between the first mode and the second mode based on a position of the support beam relative to the fixed rail. 10. The support beam of claim 1, further comprising a detent engagement assembly comprising: a swing bar housing coupled to at least one side of the support beam member; a swing bar lever pivotally mounted to the swing bar housing; at least one bias member disposed between the swing bar housing and the swing bar lever that biases the swing bar lever toward a base plate of the fixed rail; and a swing bar roller rotatably coupled to the swing bar lever, wherein the swing bar roller is operable to engage a plurality of detents positioned on the base plate of the fixed rail. 11. A cot loading and unloading system comprising: a fixed rail operable to be mounted to a floor of a cargo area of a vehicle, the fixed rail defining a passageway; a carriage coupled to the fixed rail, wherein the carriage is operable to translate along a length of the fixed rail and support a cot; and a support beam disposed within the passageway of the fixed rail, the support beam comprising a support beam member operable to translate within the passageway of the fixed rail, and also operable to support the carriage beyond a length of the fixed rail when in an extended position. 12. The cot loading and unloading system of claim 11, wherein the support beam member comprises a first support beam member side, a second support beam member side, and a top plate. 13. The cot loading and unloading system of claim 11, further comprising an end block coupled to an end of the support beam member, wherein a width of the end block is greater than a width of the support beam member. 14. The cot loading and unloading system of claim 13, further comprising a first locking assembly and a second locking assembly at opposite ends of the end block, wherein the first locking assembly and the second locking assembly are operable to both lock the support beam to the fixed rail and lock the support beam to the carriage. 15. The cot loading and unloading system of claim 14, wherein each of the first locking assembly and the second locking assembly comprises: a channel for receiving a block of the carriage; an actuation button slidably positioned within the channel and operable to receive the block of the carriage; a lock arm coupled to the actuation button; a carriage locking pin partially disposed within the channel; and a pin actuation button extending from a surface of the end block, wherein: the carriage locking pin is biased in an extended position toward the channel; when the pin actuation button contacts a face of the fixed rail: the pin actuation button moves in a direction away from the face of the fixed rail and lifts the carriage locking pin into a retracted position; and an end of the lock arm is disposed within a lock groove of the fixed rail to lock the support beam to the fixed rail; when the block of the carriage enters the channel: the block translates the actuation button in a direction away from the face of the fixed rail to lift the lock arm out of the lock groove of the fixed rail to unlock the support beam from the fixed rail; the carriage pushes the support beam out of the fixed rail such that the pin actuation button is not in contact with the face of the fixed rail; and movement of the pin actuation button in a direction toward the face of the fixed rail causes the carriage locking pin to be lowered into an extended position within the channel 

such that the carriage locking pin is disposed within a pin receiving groove of the block of the carriage. 16. The cot loading and unloading system of claim 15, wherein: the carriage locking pin has an L-shape and comprises an activation portion and a locking portion; the locking portion extends into the channel; the pin actuation button comprises a ramp surface; when the pin actuation button contacts the face of the fixed rail the ramp surface engages the activation portion to cause the carriage locking pin to be in a retracted state; and when the pin actuation button does not contact the face of the fixed rail the ramp surface lowers the activation portion to cause the carriage locking pin to be in an extended state. 17. The cot loading and unloading system of claim 11, further comprising an end block comprising an unlock button operable to: unlock a cot from the cot loading and unloading system and unlock the carriage from the fixed rail in a first mode; and unlock the cot from the cot loading and unloading system and unlock the cot from the carriage in a second mode. 18. The cot loading and unloading system of claim 17, the support beam further comprising a release rod coupled to the unlock button at a first end and a switch plate at a second end, wherein the switch plate is operable to change between the first mode and the second mode based on a position of the support beam relative to the fixed rail. 19. The cot loading and unloading system of claim 11, wherein: the support beam further comprises a detent engagement assembly comprising: a swing bar housing coupled to at least one side of the support beam member; a swing bar lever pivotally mounted to the swing bar housing; at least one bias member disposed between the swing bar housing and the swing bar lever that biases the swing bar lever toward a base plate of the fixed rail; and 

 a swing bar roller rotatably coupled to the swing bar lever; and the fixed rail further comprises a plurality of detents positioned on the base plate, wherein the swing bar roller is operable to engage the plurality of detents positioned on the base plate of the fixed rail as the support beam member translates within the passageway of the fixed rail. 20. The cot loading and unloading system of claim 19, further comprising a first ramp block and a second ramp block mounted on the base plate, wherein the first ramp block defines a first detent of the plurality of detents and the second ramp block defines a second detent and a third detent of the plurality of detents. 21. A carriage of a cot loading and unloading system, the carriage comprising: a plurality of wheels for engaging a track defined by a fixed rail of the cot loading and unloading system, wherein the carriage is operable to travel to an end of the fixed rail such that a portion of the carriage extends beyond a length of the track; and a pair of load arms for supporting a cot. 22. The carriage of claim 21, wherein: the plurality of wheels comprises a pair of first wheels and a pair of second wheels; and each wheel of the pair of first wheels has a profile to receive a protrusion of the track. 23. The carriage of claim 22, wherein each second wheel of the pair of second wheels has a profile different from the profile of the pair of first wheels. 24. The carriage of claim 22, further comprising a pair of latching mechanisms operable to latch a pair of pins of the cot, wherein the pair of latching mechanisms is operable to be opened to release the cot by at least three inputs. 25. The carriage of claim 24, wherein the at least three inputs comprises: one or more release levers at a head-end of the carriage; a button on the cot; and 

 a release button on a support beam slidably coupled to the fixed rail. 26. The carriage of claim 24, further comprising a carriage housing; a pair of release levers; a bar extending along a width of the carriage housing; a pair of lever linkages comprising a post, each lever linkage coupled to an end of the bar at a first end and coupled to a release lever of the pair of release levers at a second end; and a pair of release linkages coupled to the posts of the pair of lever linkages at a first end and the pair of latching mechanisms at a second end; wherein pulling on at least one release lever of the pair of release levers pulls the pair of lever linkages, which rotates the bar and moves the second end of the pair of release linkages in a direction toward a head-end of the carriage to unlatch the pair of latching mechanisms. 27. The carriage of claim 26, wherein when the pair of load arms are in a raised position the pair of release levers are prevented from unlatching the pair of latching mechanisms. 28. The carriage of claim 27, wherein: the bar comprises a blocking tab; the carriage further comprises: a blocking arm positioned proximate the blocking tab; a blocking linkage coupled to the blocking arm at a first end; a follower coupled to a second end of the blocking linkage; and a blocking load arm linkage coupled to an individual load arm of the pair of load arms and proximate to the follower; and when the pair of load arms are raised: the blocking arm is biased toward the head-end of the carriage such that a portion of the blocking arm is positioned relative to the blocking tab such that rotation of the bar is prevented; and when the pair of load arms are lowered: 

 the blocking load arm linkage contacts and rotates the follower; and rotation of the follower pulls the blocking linkage and the blocking arm toward the foot-end of the carriage such that the portion of the blocking arm is positioned relative to the blocking tab such that rotation of the bar is enabled. 29. The carriage of claim 28, further comprising an override button, wherein: a first end of the blocking arm is coupled to the override button; the blocking linkage comprises a slot; a second end of the blocking arm is disposed within the slot; and when the override button is pushed, the blocking arm is moved in a direction toward a foot-end of the carriage so that the bar and the blocking tab are free to rotate. 30. The carriage of claim 28, wherein: the carriage further comprises a pusher bracket, an actuator, a release arm, and an inner bar disposed within the bar and operable to rotate independently of the bar; the bar comprises an opening that exposes a portion of the inner bar; the pusher bracket is coupled to the inner bar within the opening of the bar; the release arm is coupled to the pusher bracket at a first end and the actuator at a second end; in response to receiving a signal, the actuator pushes the release arm in a direction toward the bar such that the pusher bracket rotates the inner bar, which causes the pair of latching mechanisms to become unlatched. 31. The carriage of claim 30, wherein: the carriage further comprises a carriage release flipper and a carriage release post extending from a first end of the carriage release flipper in a direction toward the fixed rail; and when an external force is applied to the carriage release post to translate the carriage release post in a direction toward the foot-end of the carriage, the carriage release flipper rotates such that a second end of the carriage release flipper pushes the release arm in a direction toward the bar such that the pusher bracket rotates the inner bar, which causes the pair of latching mechanisms to become unlatched. 32. A cot loading and unloading system comprising: a fixed rail operable to be mounted to a floor of a cargo area of a vehicle, the fixed rail defining a passageway; a carriage coupled to the fixed rail comprising a pair of load arms for supporting a cot, wherein the carriage is operable to translate along a length of the fixed rail and support a cot; and a support beam disposed within the passageway of the fixed rail, the support beam comprising a support beam member operable to translate within the passageway of the fixed rail, and also operable to support the carriage beyond a length of the fixed rail when in an extended position, wherein the carriage is prevented from traveling in a direction toward a head-end of the fixed rail when the carriage is at a fully extended position and the pair of load arms is in a lowered position. 33. The loading and unloading system of claim 32, wherein the carriage further comprises a carriage lock lever that is operable to be a lowered state to contact an edge of the fixed rail when the carriage is at the fully extended position and the pair of load arms is in the lowered position. 34. The loading and unloading system of claim 33, wherein the carriage lock lever is operable to be in a raised position when the pair of load arms is in a raised state. 35. The loading and unloading system of claim 32, The loading and unloading system of claim 12, wherein: the carriage further comprises a plate having an arcuate slot, an actuation arm, a linkage, and a carriage lock lever; one load arm of the pair of load arms comprises a pin that is disposed within the arcuate slot; the plate is coupled to a first end of the actuation arm; and the linkage is coupled to a second end of the actuation arm and to the carriage lock lever. 36. The loading and unloading system of claim 35, wherein when the pair of load arms is raised, the plate pushes the actuation arm in a direction toward the foot-end of the fixed rail to rotate the linkage and cause the carriage lock lever to be moved to a raised state. 37. The loading and unloading system of claim 35, wherein when the pair of load arms is lowered, the pin travels in the arcuate slot such that the actuation arm is pulled in a direction toward a foot-end of the fixed rail to rotate the linkage and cause the carriage lock lever to be moved to a lowered state. 38. A cot loading and unloading system comprising: a fixed rail operable to be mounted to a floor of a cargo area of a vehicle, the fixed rail defining a passageway; a carriage coupled to the fixed rail comprising a pair of load arms for supporting a cot and comprising a pair of latching mechanisms operable to latch a pair of pins of the cot, wherein the pair of latching mechanisms is operable to be opened to release the cot by at least three inputs, and wherein the carriage is operable to translate along a length of the fixed rail and support a cot; and a support beam disposed within the passageway of the fixed rail, the support beam comprising a support beam member operable to translate within the passageway of the fixed rail, and also operable to support the carriage beyond a length of the fixed rail when in an extended position, wherein the carriage is prevented from traveling in a direction toward a head-end of the fixed rail when the carriage is at a fully extended position and the pair of load arms is in a lowered position. 39. The loading and unloading system of claim 38, wherein the at least three inputs comprises: one or more release levers at a head-end of the carriage; a button on the cot; and a release button on the support beam slidably coupled to the fixed rail. 40. The loading and unloading system of claim 38, wherein: 

 the carriage further comprises a carriage lock lever that is operable to be a lowered state to contact an edge of the fixed rail when the carriage is at the fully extended position and the pair of load arms is in the lowered position; and the carriage lock lever is operable to be in a raised position when the pair of load arms is in a raised state. 41. A cot loading and unloading system comprising: a fixed rail comprising a track, wherein the fixed rail is operable to be fixed to a floor; and a carriage directly coupled to the track and operable to travel along the fixed rail on the track, wherein the carriage is further operable to travel to an extended position such that at least a portion of the carriage extends beyond a length of the fixed rail. 42. The loading and unloading system of claim 41, wherein the track comprises a rack member operable to receive a drive pinion of the carriage. 43. The loading and unloading system of claim 41, wherein: the fixed rail further comprises an end roller support section comprising safety hook, a foot-end roller, and a foot-end vertical roller; and the foot-end roller and the foot-end vertical roller are operable to support a support beam disposed in a passageway of the fixed rail. 44. The loading and unloading system of claim 43, wherein the fixed rail further comprises a central vertical roller to assist the support beam in translating within the passageway of the fixed rail. 45. The loading and unloading system of claim 41, wherein: the track comprises a first side, a second side, a rack member, a wheel guide member, and at least two guide members; the at least two guide members are disposed in opposing grooves in the second side of the track; the first side comprises a first groove and a second groove opposing the first groove; 

 the rack member is positioned in the first groove and the wheel guide member is positioned in the second groove; and the rack member comprises a rack protrusion and the wheel guide member comprises a wheel guide protrusion. 46. The loading and unloading system of claim 41, further comprising a support beam slidably disposed within a passageway of the fixed rail, wherein: the fixed rail further comprises: a base plate; and a first ramp block and a second ramp block mounted on the base plate; the first ramp block defines a first detent having a first detent switch and the second ramp block defines a second detent having a second detent switch and a third detent having a third detent switch; the first detent, the second detent and the third detent are operable to receive a swing bar roller of the support beam; and the first detent switch, the second detent switch and the third detent switch are operable to be activated by the swing bar roller to establish a position of the support beam relative to the fixed rail. 47. The loading and unloading system of claim 41, further comprising a support beam disposed within a passageway of the fixed rail, wherein the support beam is operable to translate within the passageway and support the carriage when it is in the extended position. 48. The loading and unloading system of claim 41, wherein: the fixed rail further comprises: a fastener receiver for locking a fastener of the cot to the fixed rail; and a carriage lock for locking the carriage to the fixed rail; and the carriage further comprises a pair of latch mechanisms for locking a head-end of the cot to the carriage. 49. The loading and unloading system of claim 48, wherein the fixed rail further comprises a release that is operable to unlock the carriage lock. 50. The loading and unloading system of claim 48, further comprising a support beam disposed within a passageway of the fixed rail, the support beam comprising: a support beam member; and an end block coupled to an end of the support beam member, the end block comprising an unlock button, wherein: the support beam is operable to translate within the passageway and support the carriage when it is in the extended position; and the unlock button is operable to unlock the carriage lock and the fastener receiver when the cot loading and unloading system is in a first mode; and the unlock button is operable to unlock the pair of latch mechanisms and the fastener receiver when the cot loading and unloading system is in a second mode. 51. The loading and unloading system of claim 48, wherein: the fixed rail further comprises a removable section at a foot-end of the fixed rail; the cot loading and unloading system is in the first mode when the removable section is present at the foot-end of the fixed rail; and the cot loading and unloading system is in the second mode when the removable section is removed from the foot-end of the fixed rail. 52. The loading and unloading system of claim 51, further comprising a release rod, wherein: the end block further comprises: a flipper coupled to the unlock button and the release rod; and a position lever comprising a rocker tab, wherein the rocker tab contacts the flipper; when the removable section is present on the fixed rail, the removable section presses on the position lever causing the rocker tab to press on the flipper, which further causes the release rod to be rotated in a first direction; and 

 when the removable section is removed from the foot-end of the fixed rail and the support beam member is extended beyond a top plate of the fixed rail, the position lever moves in a direction away from a base plate of the fixed rail which lifts the rocker tab away from the flipper and causes the release rod to be rotated in a second direction. 53. The loading and unloading system of claim 52, wherein: the support beam comprises: a flip-flop plate comprising a flip-flop recess; and a flip-flop tab coupled to the release rod and disposed within the flipflop plate; when the release rod is rotated in the first direction, the flip-flop tab is raised and out of the flip-flop recess; and when the release rod is rotated in the second direction, the flip-flop tab is lowered and disposed within the flip-flop recess. 54. The loading and unloading system of claim 53, wherein the fixed rail further comprises: a first linkage assembly comprising a plurality of primary links, a first input arm and a first output arm, wherein the first input arm is operable to contact the flip-flop tab when the release rod is rotated in the first direction; and a second linkage assembly comprising a plurality of secondary links, a second input arm and a second output arm, wherein the second input arm is operable to contact the flipflop tab when the release rod is rotated in the first direction. 55. The loading and unloading system of claim 54, wherein: the fixed rail further comprises a switch plate assembly comprising: a switch plate base comprising a first opening and a second opening such that the first input arm of the first linkage assembly is disposed within the first opening and the second input arm of the second linkage assembly is disposed within the second opening; an intermediate slider slidably disposed within the switch plate base and coupled to the first input arm; 

 a fastener slider slidably disposed within the switch plate base and contacting the intermediate slider; a latch slider slidably disposed within the switch plate base and contacting the fastener slider; and a carriage slider slidably disposed within the switch plate base, coupled to the intermediate slider, and operable to contact the latch slider. 56. The loading and unloading system of claim 55, wherein the fixed rail further comprises: a fastener link arm coupled to the fastener slider and operable to manipulate the fastener receiver; a carriage link arm coupled to the carriage slider and operable to manipulate the carriage lock; and a latch link arm coupled to the latch slider and operable to manipulate the pair of latch mechanisms. 57. The loading and unloading system of claim 56, wherein when the cot loading and unloading system operates in the first mode: the release rod is rotated in the first direction and the flip-flop tab is proximate the first input arm of the first linkage assembly; and when next the unlock button is pressed: the release rod is pushed in a direction toward a head-end of the fixed rail; the flip-flop tab contacts and pushes the first input arm in a direction toward the head-end of the fixed rail; the first output arm pushes the intermediate slider toward the foot-end of the fixed rail; the intermediate slider pushes the fastener link arm to unlock the fastener receiver; the intermediate slider pulls the carriage slider; and the carriage slider pulls the carriage link arm to unlock the carriage lock. 58. The loading and unloading system of claim 56, wherein: when the removable section is removed from the fixed rail the release rod is rotated in the second direction and the flip-flop tab is disposed in the flip-flop recess; when next the support beam is pushed in a direction toward, the head-end of the fixed rail the flip-flop tab passes under the first input arm toward the second input arm; and when next the support beam is fully positioned under the top plate of the fixed rail, the cot loading and unloading system operates in the second mode. 59. The loading and unloading system of claim 56, wherein when the cot loading and unloading system operates in the second mode: the release rod is rotated in the first direction and the flip-flop tab is proximate the second input arm of the first linkage assembly; when next the unlock button is pressed: the release rod is pushed in a direction toward a head-end of the fixed rail; the flip-flop tab contacts and pushes the second input arm in a direction toward the head-end of the fixed rail; the second output arm pushes the latch slider toward the foot-end of the fixed rail; and the latch slider pushes the fastener link arm to unlock the fastener receiver and pulls the latch link arm to unlock the pair of latch mechanisms. 60. A fixed rail of a cot loading and unloading system comprising a track further comprising a first side, a second side, a rack member, and a wheel guide member, wherein: the fixed rail is operable to be fixed to a floor; the rack member comprises a plurality of teeth and a rack protrusion; the wheel guide member comprises a wheel guide protrusion; the rack protrusion and the wheel guide protrusion oppose one another to receive at least one wheel of a carriage; and the rack protrusion and the wheel guide protrusion have a profile to mate with a groove of the at least one wheel. 61. A removable section of a fixed rail for a cot loading and unloading system, the removable section comprising: a first side; a second side, wherein each of the first side and the second side define a track for wheels of a carriage operable to translate on the fixed rail and the removable section; and at least one fastening mechanism for removably attaching the removable section to an end roller support section of the fixed rail. 62. The removable section of claim 61, wherein: the at least one fastening mechanism comprises: a pin comprising a threaded portion, a center portion, and a lock portion; and a fixed rail latch comprising a latch arm and an actuation arm; the threaded portion is threadedly coupled to an upper portion of the removable section; the actuation arm is coupled to the center portion of the pin; the lock portion is operable to be positioned within a lock groove of the fixed rail, the lock groove comprising a removable section lock notch; when the threaded portion is fully tightened to the upper portion of the removable section, the center portion and the actuation arm pivot the lock arm into the removable section lock notch and the lock portion is disposed within a pin hole of the end roller support section; and when the threaded portion is loosened from the upper portion of the removable section, the center portion and the actuator arm pivot the lock arm out of the removable section lock notch and the lock portion is removed from the pin hole of the end roller support section. 63. The removable section of claim 61, further comprising a tool for loosening and tightening the at least one fastening mechanism. 64. The removable section of claim 61, wherein the first side comprises a first extension arm and a second extension arm operable to receive wheels of the carriage. 65. The removable section of claim 61, wherein the track comprises a rack member on at least one of the first side and the second side. 66. The removable section of claim 65, wherein the rack member is operable to be aligned with a fixed rail rack member when the removable section is positioned on the end roller support section of the fixed rail. 67. The removable section of claim 65, wherein the first side comprises a first wheel recess for receiving one or more first wheels of the carriage and the second side comprises a second wheel recess for receiving one or more second wheels of the carriage. 68. The removable section of claim 67, wherein the first side comprises a pair of protrusions on opposing sides of the first wheel recess and the second side comprises a pair of round protrusions on opposing sides of the second wheel recess. 69. . A cot loading and unloading system comprising: a fixed rail comprising a track and an end roller support section extending beyond the track, wherein the fixed rail is operable to be fixed to a floor of a cargo area of a vehicle; and a removable section comprising: a first side; a second side, wherein each of the first side and the second side define an extended track for wheels of a carriage operable to translate on the fixed rail and the removable section; and at least one fastening mechanism for removably attaching the removable section to the end roller support section of the fixed rail. 70. The loading and unloading system of claim 69, wherein: the at least one fastening mechanism comprises: a pin comprising a threaded portion, a center portion, and a lock portion; and a fixed rail latch comprising a latch arm and an actuation arm; 

 the threaded portion is threadedly coupled to an upper portion of the removable section; the actuation arm is coupled to the center portion of the pin; the lock portion is operable to be positioned within a lock groove of the fixed rail, the lock groove comprising a removable section lock notch; when the threaded portion is fully tightened to the upper portion of the removable section, the center portion and the actuation arm pivot the lock arm into the removable section lock notch and the lock portion is disposed within a pin hole of the end roller support section; and when the threaded portion is loosened from the upper portion of the removable section, the center portion and the actuator arm pivot the lock arm out of the removable section lock notch and the lock portion is removed from the pin hole of the end roller support section. 71. The loading and unloading system of claim 69, further comprising a tool for loosening and tightening the at least one fastening mechanism. 72. The loading and unloading system of claim 69, wherein the first side comprises a first extension arm and a second extension arm operable to receive wheels of the carriage. 73. The loading and unloading system of claim 69, wherein the extended track comprises a rack member on at least one of the first side and the second side. 74. The loading and unloading system of claim 73, wherein the rack member is operable to receive a drive pinion of the carriage. 75. The loading and unloading system of claim 73, wherein the rack member is operable to be aligned with a fixed rail rack member when the removable section is positioned on the end roller support section of the fixed rail. 76. The loading and unloading system of claim 73, wherein the first side comprises a first wheel recess for receiving one or more first wheels of the carriage and the second side comprises a second wheel recess for receiving one or more second wheels of the carriage. 77. The loading and unloading system of claim 76, wherein the first side comprises a pair of protrusions on opposing sides of the first wheel recess and the second side comprises a pair of round protrusions on opposing sides of the second wheel recess. 78. The loading and unloading system of claim 69, wherein: the end roller support section comprises a safety hook, a foot-end roller, and a footend vertical roller; and the foot-end roller and the foot-end vertical roller are operable to support a support beam disposed in a passageway of the fixed rail. 79. The loading and unloading system of claim 78, wherein removal of the removable section puts the cot loading and unloading system into a manual mode. 80. A method of converting a cot loading and unloading system comprising a fixed rail, a support beam within a passageway of the fixed rail, and a removable section from an automatic mode to a manual mode, the method comprising: releasing one or more fastening mechanisms from the removable section; removing the removable section from an end roller support section of the fixed rail, thereby exposing the support beam; and pushing the support beam into the passageway of the fixed rail, thereby exposing the end roller support section comprising a safety hook. 81. A carriage of a cot loading and unloading system, the carriage comprising: a plurality of wheels for engaging a track defined by a fixed rail of the cot loading and unloading system; a power drive assembly operable to drive the carriage to travel along the track of the fixed rail; and a pair of lift arms for supporting a cot. 82. The carriage of claim 81, wherein the power drive assembly is operable to drive the carriage to an end of the fixed rail such that a portion of the carriage extends beyond a length of the track. 83. The carriage of claim 81, wherein: the power drive assembly comprises a drive motor, an axle, a motor pinion, and a drive pinion; the axle is coupled to the drive motor and the axle; the axle is coupled to the motor pinion such that rotation of the axle by the drive motor rotates the motor pinion; and teeth of the motor pinion engage teeth of the drive pinion such that rotation of the motor pinion causes rotation of the drive pinion to drive the carriage on the track. 84. The carriage of claim 83, wherein the teeth of the drive pinion are operable to engage teeth of a rack member such that rotation of the drive pinion causes linear motion of the carriage on the track. 85. The carriage of claim 84, wherein: the plurality of wheels comprises a pair of first wheels and a pair of second wheels; and each wheel of the pair of first wheels has a profile to receive a protrusion of the track. 86. The carriage of claim 85, wherein the protrusion has a V-shape and the profile has a W- shape. 87. The carriage of claim 85, each second wheel of the pair of second wheels has a flat profile or a grooved profile. 88. The carriage of claim 81, further comprising at least one cleaning assembly mounted relative to at least one wheel of the plurality of wheels, the at least one cleaning assembly operable to clean the at least one wheel. 89. A cot loading and unloading system comprising: a fixed rail comprising a track, wherein the fixed rail is operable to be fixed to a floor; and a carriage directly coupled to the track, the carriage comprising: a plurality of wheels that engage the track of the fixed rail; a power drive assembly operable to drive the carriage to travel along the track of the fixed rail; and a pair of lift arms for supporting a cot. 90. The loading and unloading system of claim 89, wherein the power drive assembly is operable to drive the carriage to a foot-end of the fixed rail such that a majority of the carriage extends beyond a length of the track. 91. The loading and unloading system of claim 89, wherein: the power drive assembly comprises a drive motor, an axle, a motor pinion, and a drive pinion; the axle is coupled to the drive motor and the axle; the axle is coupled to the motor pinion such that rotation of the axle by the drive motor rotates the motor pinion; and teeth of the motor pinion engage teeth of the drive pinion such that rotation of the motor pinion causes rotation of the drive pinion to drive the carriage on the track. 92. The loading and unloading system of claim 91, wherein: the track comprises a rack member having teeth; and the teeth of the drive pinion engage the teeth of the rack member. 93. The loading and unloading system of claim 92, wherein: the track comprises a first side, a second side, a wheel guide member, and at least two guide members; the at least two guide members oppose one another at the second side of the track; the rack member comprises a rack protrusion and the wheel guide member comprises a wheel guide protrusion; and 

 the rack protrusion and the wheel guide protrusion oppose one another at the first side of the track. 94. The loading and unloading system of claim 93, wherein: the plurality of wheels comprises a pair of first wheels and a pair of second wheels; and each wheel of the pair of first wheels has a profile to receive a protrusion of the track. 95. The loading and unloading system of claim 94, wherein the protrusion has a V-shape and the profile has a W-shape. 96. The loading and unloading system of claim 95, each second wheel of the pair of second wheels has a flat profile or a grooved profile. 97. A cot loading and unloading system comprising: a fixed rail comprising a track, wherein: the fixed rail is operable to be fixed to a floor; the track comprises a first side, a second side, a rack member, a wheel guide member, and at least two guide members; the at least two guide members are provided at the second side of the track; the rack member and the wheel guide member are provided at the first side of the track; and the rack member comprises a rack protrusion and the wheel guide member comprises a wheel guide protrusion; a carriage comprising a power drive assembly comprising a drive motor, an axle, a motor pinion, and a drive pinion, wherein: the axle is coupled to the drive motor and the axle; the axle is coupled to the motor pinion such that rotation of the axle by the drive motor rotates the motor pinion; teeth of the motor pinion engage teeth of the drive pinion such that rotation of the motor pinion causes rotation of the drive pinion; and 

 the teeth of the drive pinion engage teeth of the rack member such that rotation of the motor pinion causes the carriage to travel along the fixed rail on the track; and a support beam disposed within a passageway of the fixed rail, wherein the support beam is operable to translate within the passageway and support the carriage when it is in an extended position beyond a length of the fixed rail. 98. The loading and unloading system of claim 97, wherein: the carriage further comprises a pair of first wheels and a pair of second wheels; and each wheel of the pair of first wheels has a profile to receive a protrusion of the track. 99. The loading and unloading system of claim 98, wherein the protrusion has a V-shape and the profile has a W-shape. 100. The loading and unloading system of claim 99, each second wheel of the pair of second wheels has a flat profile or a grooved profile. 101. A system comprising: a cot loading and unloading system comprising: a fixed rail comprising a track, wherein the fixed rail is operable to be fixed to a floor of a cargo area of a vehicle; a carriage comprising a carriage wireless chip, wherein the carriage is coupled to the track and operable to travel along the fixed rail on the track; and a master controller comprising a master wireless chip; a cot comprising a cot wireless chip, wherein: the master wireless chip is operable to transmit a broadcast address to the cot; the cot wireless chip is operable to receive the broadcast address and transmit a cot address signal; the carriage wireless chip is operable to receive the cot address signal and transmit a strength signal based on the cot address signal; and 

 the master controller is operable to receive the strength signal from the carriage wireless chip. 102. The system of claim 101, wherein the master controller is operable to receive a strength signal from one or more additional cots, and selects an individual cot among the cot and the one or more additional cots for wireless pairing based on the strength signal from the cot and the strength signal from the one or more additional cots. 103. The system of claim 101, wherein: the fixed rail further comprises rail wireless chip; the rail wireless chip is operable to receive the broadcast address and transmit a cot address signal; the rail wireless chip is operable to receive the cot address signal and transmit a strength signal based on the cot address signal; and the master controller is operable to receive the strength signal from the rail wireless chip. 104. The system of claim 103, wherein the master controller is operable to receive a strength signal from one or more additional cots, and selects an individual cot of the one or more additional cots for wireless pairing based on the strength signal from the cot and the strength signal from the one or more additional cots as transmitted by the cot wireless chip and the rail wireless chip. 105. The system of claim 103, wherein the master controller comprises an electronic display operable to display one or more status indicators of a paired cot. 106. A method of operating a cot loading and unloading system comprising a fixed rail comprising a track, wherein the fixed rail is operable to be fixed to a floor of a cargo area of a vehicle, a carriage, wherein the carriage is coupled to the track and operable to travel along the fixed rail on the track, and a master controller, the method comprising: wirelessly transmitting, by the master controller, a broadcast address to one or more cots;

The method of claim 106, wherein the individual cot selected for pairing is associated with a strength signal of the one or more strength signals having a largest strength value. 108. The method of claim 107, wherein the one or more strength signals each comprise a RSSI value. 109. The method of claim 106, wherein the one or more strength signals are received from the carriage. 110. The method of claim 106, wherein the one or more strength signals are received from the fixed rail. 111. The method of claim 106, wherein the one or more strength signals are received from the carriage and the fixed rail. 112. The method of claim 111, wherein the individual cot selected for pairing is associated with a strength signal of the one or more strength signals received from the carriage and the fixed rail having a largest strength value. 113. The method of claim 112, the one or more strength signals each comprise a RSSI value. 114. The method of claim 112, further comprising displaying one or more status indicators of a paired cot on a display screen of the master controller. 115.  a fixed rail comprising a track, wherein the fixed rail is operable to be fixed to a floor; a carriage comprising at least one load arm, wherein the carriage is coupled to the track and operable to travel along the fixed rail on the track; and a master controller operable to be communicatively coupled to the fixed rail and the carriage; and a cot comprising a base and at least one sensor mounted on the base at a position such that the at least one sensor is operable to detect a presence and an absence of the at least one load arm proximate the base. 116. The system of claim 115, wherein the at least one sensor is an ultrasonic sensor. 117. The system of claim 117, wherein the pairing process comprises detecting the presence of the at least one load arm and then the absence of the at least one load arm using the at least one sensor. 119. The system of claim 117, wherein the pairing process comprises sequentially: detecting an upper edge of the at least one load arm using the at least one sensor; detecting a lower edge of the at least one load arm using the at least one sensor; and detecting the absence of the at least one load arm using the at least one sensor. 120. The system of claim 119, wherein the pairing process further comprises receiving a load arm contact switch activation signal.
